summary: Calling `AnimationMixer.remove_animation_library(name : StringName)` from @tool script doesn't remove the library from `animation_libraries`

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.5.1.stable.mono.official [f62fdbde1]
v4.6.dev4.mono.official [bd2ca13c6]

### System information

Linux

### Issue description

I want to remove and create `AnimationLibrary`'s through a toolscript in my project for a specific workflow.

However after removing a random empty `AnimationLibrary` I made using `AnimationMixer.remove_animation_library(name)` I started getting errors printed in my console.

The first time I click `Manage Animations` I get the following messages printed in my console:
```
Can't find  mirrored  in mixer
Can't find  _sword  in mixer
```
(the names of two `AnimationLibrary`'s I removed via my toolscript)

Then every subsequent time I click `Manage Animations` I get my console spammed with:
```
ERROR: scene/animation/animation_mixer.cpp:267 - Condition "p_animation_library.is_null()" is true. Returning: StringName()
```
exactly 52 times, each time I click `Manage Animations`.

After checking this block out in the source code:
```gdscript
Ref<AnimationLibrary> AnimationMixer::get_animation_library(const StringName &p_name) const {
    for (const AnimationLibraryData &lib : animation_libraries) {
        if (lib.name == p_name) {
            return lib.library;
        }
    }
    ERR_FAIL_V(Ref<AnimationLibrary>());
}
```
I can only assume `get_animation_library()` is called whenever I click `Manage Animations` (presumably to populate the editor popup window), and for some reason `animation_libraries` still contains `mirrored` and `_sword`.
This is despite these two animation libraries **not** existing inside the `AnimationMixer`'s `library` dictionary.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Download MRP
2. Run remove_library_toolscript.gd with `dummy_player.tscn` open.
3. Click `Animation` -> `Manage Animations` on `AnimationTree`
4. `_library_to_remove` still exists, despite being removed. Click the name, as if you were to rename it, then press enter to confirm it's name as the one that already exists.
6. Click `Animation` -> `Manage Animations` again.

You will find this now prints: `ERROR: scene/animation/animation_mixer.cpp:267 - Condition "p_animation_library.is_null()" is true. Returning: StringName()`
If you close the project and re-open it, then click `Animation` -> `Manage Animations` again, you will get a print message saying `Can't find    in mixer`.

This isn't completely accurate to what I see in my own project, but it is definitely the closest.

I think it is something to do with calling `AnimationTree.remove_animation_library(name)`.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)
[RemoveLibrary.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23838107/RemoveLibrary.zip)

summary: Fix project path resetting when canceling folder browse in project manager

description.text:
### Issue
When creating a new project, if the user:
1. Opens the project creation dialog (default path A is shown)
2. Clicks "Browse" and selects a different path B
3. Clicks "Browse" again and presses "Cancel"

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/67659b4d-e989-489c-a989-a9ae295e8d66

The project path incorrectly reverts to the default path A instead of preserving the user-selected path B.

### Root Cause
In `ProjectDialog::show_dialog()`, the path resetting logic is executed unconditionally after the folder browser closes, regardless of whether the user confirmed or canceled the selection.

### Fix
For new project creation mode (`MODE_NEW`), skip the path resetting logic when the dialog is shown without confirmation (`p_is_confirmed=false`). This preserves the previously selected path when the user cancels the folder browser.

### Testing
- Reproduced the issue before the fix
- Verified the path is now correctly preserved after canceling the browse dialog
- Tested creating a project with the new behavior - works as expected

**Note**: This is a minimal fix. A more comprehensive refactoring could separate the path initialization logic from the dialog display logic for better maintainability.

summary: Wayland: Unify key handling logic

description.text:
Previously we had different logic for direct key presses and client-side key repetition, as one queued up input events and the other dispatched them directly (client-side key repetition is run from the main thread).

I kinda figured out that this difference doesn't really matter, as we can queue them up before the thread message dispatching logic. That's exactly what we do now, which allows us to make a single method for both of them, making the code much clearer and simplifying future maintenance.

This patch also includes a tiny fixup in the compose logic, which checks for the validity of the generated key event before actually working with it. The cases in which we can end up with an invalid reference are very few, so it's not the end of the world, but it's still absolutely a good idea to check, to avoid nasty surprises down the line.

---

This is purely a code structure change (behavior should be identical), but it makes adding new features a lot easier, including the bump to `wl_keyboard` version 10 (server-side key repetition).

summary: Godot 4.6.dev4 | Editor crashes if there are cyclical node references via Arrays

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in
- v4.6.dev4.official [bd2ca13c6]

Not reproducible in
- v4.6.dev3.official [9d84f3d13]


### System information

Godot v4.6.dev4.mono - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.21025.10016) - AMD Ryzen 7 3800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 31.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

If you have 2 instances of a script which have an array, and these instances reference each other via the array, or an instance references itself via the array, the editor immediately crashes.

I found this after opening an existing project in v4.6.dev4, and upon opening the scene for my player, the editor crashed each time.

The player has several instances of a teleport beam, but can only cast one teleport beam at a time.
Each teleport beam on the player has an array for the other teleport beams, and it checks to make sure that no beams are being cast yet. Left and right hands get their own teleport beams in VR.

This does not happen using NodePaths, it only crashes with direct references.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project
2. Create a new scene
3. Add a child node
4. Create a script, C# or GDScript
5. Add an export array variable with the same type or class_name as your script
6. Recompile if using C#
7. Select the child node
8. Set the script on the child node to the one just created
9. Save the scene, we are about to crash the editor.
10. In the inspector, open the array variable and add a reference to the node itself.
11. The editor freezes for a few seconds, and then crashes.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

This MRP uses GDScript.

[array-crash-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23836619/array-crash-mrp.zip)

1. Open the project
2. Open repro.tscn
3. Select the node in the scene tree
4. In the inspector, open the array, and add a reference to itself
5. Editor crashes

summary: 2D Panel Inspector StyleBoxFlat Panel Checkbox stuck in disabled

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.5.1 stable

### System information

Windows 11 Pro - Godot v4.5.1.stable - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X (Zen 5 Architecture)

### Issue description

I follow a turoria to learn godot and currently Im making a HUD. "Godot 4 3D Platformer Lesson 18: Coins Heads-Up Display (HUD)!" and is by a channel called "BornCG" on YouTube. In his version it worked in mine the bug happened though: 

The Panel-checkbox for the style type StyleBoxFlat (which is set as default) is stuck on flase and can't be activated for a Panel under a CanvasLayer. Needed to switch styles back and forth to work around it. 

Error-Message: "Toggling the checkbox is disabled for Resource properties. Modify the property using the resource picker instead."

### Steps to reproduce

I made a new scene and added a CanvasLayer which I named HUD, after which I added a Panel as a child of HUD. When I opened the Inspector of the Panel and opened the Styles under Theme Overrides, the StyleBoxFlat was allready set, which is the style type I need. The checkbox Panel was set to false though and I couldn't set it to true, also not when I expanded the StyleBoxFlat. I got an error message which said: "Toggling the checkbox is disabled for Resource properties. Modify the property using the resource picker instead." So I needed to set the Style to a different one and then back to StyleBoxFlat to work around it, then the Panel checkbox works. This is also repeatable, when I create a new Panel. 
In the tutorial the style was not set, so either that is the bug, or that the panel-checkbox should be set to true by default.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Editor: Fix infinite appending of docks without slots to config

description.text:
## Summary

Fixes #113281

This PR fixes a bug where docks without default slots were being infinitely appended to the editor layout config file.

## Root Cause

The clearing loop at lines 574-601 only clears dock slots 0 through `DOCK_SLOT_MAX` (generating config keys `dock_1` through `dock_15`). However, docks without default slots use index `-1`, which corresponds to the config key `dock_0`. This key was never cleared, so closed docks kept getting appended infinitely on each save.

## Changes Made

Added code after line 601 to explicitly clear the `dock_0` config key before saving closed docks, matching the behavior of regular dock slots.

## Testing

Tested with the reproduction steps provided in the issue:
1. Created a plugin with an `EditorDock` that has no default slot
2. Enabled the plugin
3. Pressed Ctrl+S multiple times
4. Verified that `dock_0` in `.godot/editor/editor_layout.cfg` no longer accumulates duplicate entries

The fix ensures that the special `-1` slot is properly cleared on each save, preventing the infinite appending behavior.

summary: MacOS: Fix focus grab warning on macOS when running game in embedded mode.

description.text: Fixes focus grab warning on macOS when running game in embedded mode. ## Issue When running a game with "Embed Game on Next Play" enabled on macOS, the following warning is printed: "This control can't grab focus. Use set_focus_mode() and set_focus_behavior_recursive() to allow a control to get focus." ## Root Cause `EmbeddedProcessMacOS` is explicitly configured with `set_focus_mode(FOCUS_NONE)` in its constructor, while `GameView` unconditionally calls `grab_focus()` on it when starting the game instance. The focus is intentionally delegated to the child `layer_host` which uses `FOCUS_ALL`. However, `grab_focus()` on the parent process still triggers the warning even though this is the intended design on macOS. ## Solution Add a focus mode check before calling `grab_focus()` in `GameView::_play_pressed()` and `GameView::_instance_starting()`. This prevents the warning when the control is explicitly set to not accept focus, matching the macOS-specific implementation's design. ### Changes Made - Added `if (embedded_process->get_focus_mode_with_override() != FOCUS_NONE)` guard before `embedded_process->grab_focus()` calls in `game_view.cpp` ## Testing - Tested on macOS: warning no longer appears when launching game in embedded mode - Verified other platforms (Windows/Linux) remain unaffected - Confirmed focus behavior still works correctly in embedded game window

summary: Support unary '+' in Variant parser

description.text: This adds support for parsing '+' in Variants. Currently, in an exported property, if you type `-1` it correctly assigns `-1`. However, if you assign `+1`, it is rejected as erroneous. I believe that this small change should fix it. Floats are later parsed with [`as_double()`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/7ed0b6167618f9c8c5df87c7e83da6003c081368/core/string/string_buffer.h#L154) which calls [`String::to_float`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/7ed0b6167618f9c8c5df87c7e83da6003c081368/core/string/ustring.cpp#L2574) which calls [`built_in_strtod`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/7ed0b6167618f9c8c5df87c7e83da6003c081368/core/string/ustring.cpp#L2352) which [handles `+` signs](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/7ed0b6167618f9c8c5df87c7e83da6003c081368/core/string/ustring.cpp#L2424).

summary: Docks without slots will be infinitely appended to dock list

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.6 7ed0b61
Not sure if regression, might be not, despite the recent changes.

### System information

W10

### Issue description

There is a special dock slot for docks that don't have default slot.
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/7ed0b6167618f9c8c5df87c7e83da6003c081368/editor/docks/editor_dock_manager.cpp#L684-L685
Only docks that have `default_slot` set to NONE can end up there.

The fun part is the saving code.
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/7ed0b6167618f9c8c5df87c7e83da6003c081368/editor/docks/editor_dock_manager.cpp#L647-L655
The editor appends closed docks in their original slot. For other slots, the dock list is cleared in the loop above. However the special "-1" slot is never cleared. Hence this code will just append stuff infinitely...

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/11692de3-14bb-4fab-8d38-f0bbf0b0ad45

### Steps to reproduce

1. Add this plugin to your project
```
@tool
extends EditorPlugin

var dock: EditorDock

func _enter_tree() -> void:
	dock = EditorDock.new()
	dock.title = "AAAAAA"
	add_dock(dock)

func _exit_tree() -> void:
	remove_dock(dock)
	dock.queue_free()
```
2. Enable it
3. Press Ctrl+S
4. Open `.godot/editor/editor_layout.cfg` and check `dock_0` field.
5. Keep pressing Ctrl+S

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Use the script above.

summary: Exporting Variant initialized as Array(or certain types) results in Nil

description.text: ### Tested versions Reproducible in 4.5 and above. Variants can't be exported until v4.5. ### System information Godot v4.6.dev (7ed0b6167) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - i7-14700K (28 threads) - 31.76 GiB memory ### Issue description A variable can be exported as a Variant as follows: ``` @export var test1 = 1 as Variant @export var test2 = "test" as Variant @export var test3 = [1, 2] as Variant ``` the first two variables are exported correctly as Variants. But the third variable is exported as a Nil type. whereas it was supposed to be exported like this: It happens to basically any nullable type like Array, PackedByteArray, Dictionary, etc.. Another thing to note is that whenever you change the type in editor from a non-nullable type to a nullable type, the nullable type always gets assigned to Nil. But when i change that variable to Nil type and change it to Array or any nullable type, the default value gets assigned correctly. Basically, exporting or editing nullable types (Array, Dictionary, etc.) as Variant in Godot causes them to incorrectly default to Nil, unlike basic types which export correctly. ### Steps to reproduce Attach a script to any node in the scene. Issue while creating: Try exporting Array or Dictionary variable like: ``` @export var test3 = [1, 2] as Variant @export var test4 = {} as Variant ``` See the output in editor -> it will be null, instead it should be represented as valid objects of respective types. Issue while modifying: ``` @export var test1 = 1 as Variant @export var test2 = "test" as Variant ``` Change the type of the variables in editor (using the pen icon) to Array or Dictionary. You will see null, instead it should be represented as default values of respective types. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [reproc_project_4.6.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23824098/reproc_project_4.6.zip)

summary: VoxelGI tooltip displays incorrect cell size

description.text: ### Tested versions Godot v4.6.dev4 ### System information Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (Intel Corporation; 32.0.101.7076) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-1135G7 @ 2.40GHz (8 threads) - 15.80 GiB memory ### Issue description Trying to reduce light leaking of `VoxelGI`. ### Issue description

On the project I'm working on, I pushed a change that added a signal to a script and connected the signal in a scene via editor. summary: GLTF: Add `export_get_property_list` to GLTFDocumentExtension

description.text: This PR adds a new callback method that runs before the entire export process begins, which returns the `PropertyInfo` list of the properties relevant for that class. This allows extensions to provide context-sensitive options without cluttering the menu. For example, PR #112866 uses this feature to show this dropdown menu when there are MultiMeshInstance3D nodes in the scene, but hide it when none exist in the scene: I also fixed a bug where the extension name wasn't considering the GDScript class name when it should. summary: Fix stale error highlighting when navigating script history

description.text:
Fixes: #113147

This PR fixes an issue where scripts were not being re-validated when navigating through the editor history. This caused error highlights to persist even after the underlying issue was resolved in another file.

I added a validate() call in ScriptEditor::_update_history_pos to ensure the script is re-analyzed upon history navigation, consistent with the behavior when switching tabs manually in ScriptEditor::_go_to_tab.

summary: Add source path filtering to encrypted and delta-encoded exports

description.text:
> [!IMPORTANT]
> This currently depends on (and therefore includes) #112764. To see the actual relevant changes, open just the last commit, or see [this](https://github.com/mihe/godot/compare/export-cleanup...mihe:godot:export-source-path-filtering) diff.

The filter text fields that can be found in both the "Encryption" and "Patching" tabs in the export dialog are currently a bit strange, as they operate solely on the exported/remapped paths of resources. This means that if you want to (for example) include/exclude all the textures that you have in your `res://textures/` folder, you will be unable to do so, because once exported all textures will typically end up in a flat structure, with a path in the format of `res://.godot/imported/[name].[ext]-[hash].ctex`.

This fixes that, with the help of the `source_path` property/parameter introduced in #112764, by also checking if the filters apply to the source path.

Note that this is technically a breaking change, since the encryption filters will now potentially apply to paths that maybe they weren't meant to. I do however think that this is a worthwhile breakage, and I have a hard time imagining there being many projects out there that would be impacted by this.

description.text:
This is a port of @lawnjelly's work on the "Vital Updates Only" Editor Setting feature. Looking through some older issues I saw several comments from @Calinou suggesting this port could resolve the issues and was desired.

Replaces Editor Setting "Update Continuously" with new Enum, "Update Priority", add adds "Vital_Only" option, which stops many in-editor redraw_requests.

Resolves:
* https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/39758
* https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/76734
* https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/10042

Implements:
* https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/942

Ports:
* https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/53463

Currently missing from this port: The Editor Caret Blink settings.

Port is currently as direct as possible, but I'd be interested in any feedback on how this feature could be made more effective, or fit better into the 4.x ecosystem. 

To test, create a new project, and create a frequently updating node, such as a Particle Spawner. Go to Editor Settings, and change "Update Priority" from it's default Update_When_Changed to Update_Vital_Only, and you should see the scene redraw much less frequently, if not freeze entirely.

summary: Windows: Make Direct3D 12 the default RD driver for new projects

description.text:
- Implements and closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12234

Direct3D 12 support was added for the Windows platform in Godot 4.3 by @RandomShaper and further refined in later releases by @DarioSamo (both sponsored by W4 Games, so I'll obama-medal-meme my company a bit).
In the 4.6 release cycle, @blueskythlikesclouds focused on fixing known issues with Direct3D 12, with the aim of making it the default RenderingDevice driver for Windows (this time sponsored by the Godot Foundation :1st_place_medal:).

Why? Mainly because Vulkan support on Windows is giving us a lot of headache, with GPU drivers often being poorly maintained compared to their Direct3D 12 counterparts. We've seen many regressions in Nvidia and AMD drivers that would outright break Godot and games released with it (e.g. #109378 still ongoing). There are other issues such as Vulkan implicit layers which force us to use [hacks](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/9dd6c4dbac70b28e8156255c3a2b78722772b036/main/main.cpp#L2081-L2097) to work around their harmful impact on Godot.

It's understandable, most Windows games use modern APIs target Direct3D 12 on Windows, and thus these are the drivers GPU vendors put most work and testing into.

So now that Direct3D 12 in Godot has more or less feature parity with Vulkan (see below), we want to try using it as the default option. See https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12234 for more arguments in favor of defaulting to Direct3D 12.

Some important aspects:
- *Edit:* This only impacts **new projects** created in 4.6 or later. To use this new default in pre-existing Godot 4.5 projects, you should set the `rendering/rendering_device/driver.windows` project setting to `d3d12` manually.
- Vulkan is still available and can be selected via the `rendering/rendering_device/driver.windows` project setting, or `--rendering-driver vulkan` on the command line.
- ~This PR attempts to preserve the behavior of pre-existing projects by explicitly setting the driver to `vulkan` if it was unconfigured (default) prior to upgrading to 4.6. (Uses the approach added by @KoBeWi in #112607.) So by default, only new projects should default to `d3d12`.~
  * *Edit:* Took a different approach in the end with similar results. Now `vulkan` stays the default for this setting, but `d3d12` is configured automatically for new projects created in 4.6.
  * When upgrading from 4.5, you should therefore change `rendering/rendering_device/driver.windows` to `d3d12` if you want to use the new default (recommended).
- Compiling from source for Windows will now require downloading the dependencies needed by Direct3D 12. You can do it by running the `misc/scripts/install_d3d12_sdk_windows.py` script. Alternatively, you can compile with `d3d12=no` if you don't need it for your development work.
- For PCVR users, we're aware that the Direct3D 12 backend doesn't have full parity with Vulkan yet (missing VRS and foveated rendering IIUC). So if that's a problem for your needs, you can switch back to Vulkan with the aforementioned project setting.
- When using Wine to run Windows games on Linux/macOS, note that the system versions of Wine don't support Direct3D 12 by default. Godot should fallback gracefully to Vulkan in that case. On Steam/Proton, it should use Direct3D 12 by default through vkd3d-proton.

summary: Exclusive Fullscreen Issues With Main Scene Reload

description.text:
### Tested versions

### System information

### Issue description

In my game I am setting exclusive fullscreen via settings at startup. This all works as intended. I've been having an issue where when I try to reload the main scene in the editor that it will I assume not have focus, and require waiting some amount of time or tabbing in and out for processing / input handling to resume.

After debugging and looking through Godot source code I believe I've found a way to reproduce it. In the reload case it looks like in EditorRun::stop() it simply kills the pids. My guess is that it doesn't shutdown the windows properly, so when we reopened there's some issue with what window Windows thinks is has exclusive fullscreen. I was able to reproduce this issue by killing the PID of my game directly which produces the same issue as reloading.


### Steps to reproduce

Steps:
1. Play
2. Reload (Sometimes this works? So please try a few times)
3. Observe that inputs and processing are no longer being provided to the window
4. Close normally
5. Play
6. Kill window PID
7. Observe the same results
8. Close normally
9. Play normally
10. Observe that these issues don't occur

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I provided a project that displays this problem. It simply has a button that changes its opacity when hovered and prints out every process step that it's processing. 

[exclusive_fullscreen_bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23780211/exclusive_fullscreen_bug.zip)

summary: Switching between back and front camera freezes the app

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.6dev [9dd6c4dbac70b28e8156255c3a2b78722772b036]


### System information

Godot v4.6.dev (59a3179c3) - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8180) - AMD Ryzen 9 9900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads) - 61.66 GiB memory

### Issue description

Switching between back and front camera freezes the app

### Steps to reproduce

1. Build and install this project onto phone
1. Open once and allow camera permissions
1. Close app
1. Open again
1. Try to switch between back camera and front camera
1. Observe that the app has frozen and does not respond to input

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

https://github.com/shiena/godot-camerafeed-demo
